jQuery Was Once King — So Why Did It Fall Off?

A while back someone asked me why the industry mostly recommends Vue, React, and Angular these days instead of jQuery — even though they still use jQuery at work every day. They were curious what's actually behind that shift. So I wrote this post to talk through it: how jQuery rose to the top, and why it eventually got left behind.
The Fake jQuery Trick That'll Have Your Coworkers Scratching Their Heads

A fun little trick to confuse your coworkers — make them think they're using real jQuery when it's actually just plain JavaScript. We'll fake out the `$()` selector, the `.on()` event helper, and even `hide()` / `show()`, all with vanilla JS. A reminder, too, that jQuery itself is just JavaScript under the hood.
